# Sprint Planning Checklist

Use this checklist to ensure thorough preparation and execution of sprint planning sessions.

## Pre-Planning (Before the Meeting)
- [ ] **Product Manager**: Backlog is groomed and prioritized
- [ ] **Product Manager**: User stories have clear acceptance criteria
- [ ] **UX Designer**: Designs and wireframes are finalized for upcoming work
- [ ] **Scrum Master**: Sprint planning meeting scheduled with all required attendees
- [ ] **Scrum Master**: Previous sprint velocity and capacity reviewed
- [ ] **Developers**: Previous sprint work is completed or carry-over identified
- [ ] **Data Analyst**: Analytics instrumentation needs identified for new features

## During Planning
- [ ] **Scrum Master**: Review sprint goal and previous sprint outcomes
- [ ] **Product Manager**: Present prioritized backlog items
- [ ] **UX Designer**: Walk through designs for UI-related stories
- [ ] **Developers**: Ask clarifying questions and identify technical dependencies
- [ ] **Team**: Estimate story points or effort for each item
- [ ] **Team**: Confirm capacity and account for time off or other commitments
- [ ] **Team**: Select items that fit capacity and align with sprint goal
- [ ] **Data Analyst**: Confirm tracking and metrics plan for sprint deliverables
- [ ] **Scrum Master**: Document sprint commitments and blockers

## Post-Planning
- [ ] **Scrum Master**: Update project board with sprint backlog
- [ ] **Project Manager**: Align sprint commitments with overall project timeline
- [ ] **Release Manager**: Note any release dependencies or commitments
- [ ] **Team**: All committed stories moved to "Ready" status
- [ ] **Scrum Master**: Sprint goal communicated to stakeholders

## Notes
- Target: Complete planning within 2 hours for a 2-week sprint
- Ensure everyone understands Definition of Done
- Flag any cross-team dependencies immediately
